harvestedTerms.summary: IBM's Granite 4.1 technical walkthrough covers a multi-stage pre-training pipeline, LLM-as-Judge SFT quality control, GRPO-based reinforcement learning, and long-context extension techniques. Several trackable concepts emerge including the LLM-as-Judge evaluation framework, GRPO (a specific RL algorithm), long-context extension (LCE), and data annealing.

article.title: Amazon Earnings, Trainium and Commodity Markets, Additional Amazon Notes

harvestedTerms.summary: Article covers Amazon's Q1 2026 earnings with focus on Trainium (Amazon's custom AI chip), the shift from training to inference and agents, and broader implications for AI infrastructure. Key themes: custom silicon for inference, commodity dynamics in AI hardware markets, and agentic workloads driving chip demand.

article.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6

harvestedTerms.summary: Podcast episode covering ChatGPT Images 2.0 for text-in-image generation, Chinese model releases (Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi K2.6 1T MoE, MiniMax M2.7), Google Deep Research Max with MCP, Claude Mythos security incidents, SpaceX-Cursor deal, Cerebras IPO filing, and synthetic media policy topics.

article.title: Open-world evaluations for measuring frontier AI capabilities

harvestedTerms.summary: Article introduces CRUX, a collaborative project for open-world AI evaluations—long, messy, real-world tasks that go beyond standard benchmarks. Key concepts include open-world evaluations as a distinct evaluation paradigm, the CRUX organization itself, and the iOS app publication experiment as an example of agentic capability assessment.

Article covers Codex CLI 0.128.0's new /goal feature, which implements a loop-until-goal-achieved pattern with token budget management, referencing the 'Ralph loop' concept and automatic prompt injection for goal evaluation.

definitionReviews.reason: The CRUX paper on open-world evaluations reinforces the existing definition's point that building good evals is hard and that benchmark saturation is a real problem. The new material (open-world evals, CRUX project) extends the concept but doesn't contradict or materially update the core definition enough to require a rewrite.
